data_pipeline.description: How data flows between Viridian and KRAMOS

ownership.mastery_tracking: Viridian (source of truth; KRAMOS writes results)
ownership.generated_packets: KRAMOS (Viridian reads and delivers to students)

agreements.versioning: Git commits to this file; both systems log updates with timestamps
agreements.access: Shared GitHub repo (kyle-kramer-collaboration); both systems have write access

next_actions.action: Viridian: Define student-profile data format (for KRAMOS to consume)
KRAMOS: Review student-profile schema; confirm packet-engine can consume it
next_actions.kramos_note: Reviewed 2026-06-09. Consumable with two amendments (dual reading_level cefr+us; license field on packet sources). See kramos_consumer_notes. Date is a target; Kramer ratifies hard dates given Summer teaching load.

next_actions.action: Viridian: Finalize The Bridge JSON (this file); add to Viridian repo
KRAMOS: Wire packet-engine to read from The Bridge
next_actions.depends_on: The Bridge finalized; profile payload location + ready-signal defined (see kramos_consumer_notes); packet engine v1 exists
next_actions.kramos_note: Blocked on two missing pieces in v0: where profile payloads live (KRAMOS prefers files-in-repo for Phase 1) and a per-domain ready-signal. Read cadence: every listener fire (2h) in Phase 1, event-driven once packet engine v1 emits. Date is a target; Kramer ratifies.

next_actions.action: Viridian: Wire Mastery Tracker to write results to The Bridge

kramos_consumer_notes.verdict: v0 consumable with amendments below; KRAMOS adopts the Bridge and treats this file as source of truth

amendments_requested.issue: v0 enum (5th-grade | 7th-grade | college) is US-grade-band only. KRAMOS differentiates on CEFR (A1-C1) + 95/5 comprehensibility; US-grade readability formulas misbehave on ESL text.
amendments_requested.proposal: Carry both: reading_level_cefr and reading_level_us. Commit a shared mapping table to the repo. LHUM400 is college-level English regardless; CEFR axis matters for ESL-adjacent cohorts.

amendments_requested.issue: No license field. KRAMOS educational-content rule requires source + license + TASL attribution, and excludes CC-BY-NC from materials students pay for.
amendments_requested.proposal: Add license:string to each source entry. Packets will carry attribution.

gaps_blocking_consumer_wiring.detail: Bridge carries the profile schema but not the data and no pointer to where payloads live. Ownership says KRAMOS reads, but reads from where.
gaps_blocking_consumer_wiring.kramos_preference: Files-in-repo for Phase 1 (e.g. profiles/<domain>/<student-id>.json) — keeps the loop inside Git, no auth surface, no uptime dependency. Move to a Viridian endpoint later if volume demands.

gaps_blocking_consumer_wiring.detail: No flag tells KRAMOS new profiles exist without diffing payloads.
gaps_blocking_consumer_wiring.proposal: Add per-domain profiles_ready:bool + profiles_updated_iso. KRAMOS checks on each listener fire (every 2h).

agreements_from_kramos.bridge_timing: Viridian writes real-time. KRAMOS reads every listener fire (2h) in Phase 1; upgrades to event-driven once packet engine v1 emits. No premature real-time wiring while KRAMOS has nothing to write back.
agreements_from_kramos.phase1_stress_test: Yes. One synthetic profile through the full loop before June 16 real students: Viridian posts a fake profile -> KRAMOS generates one real 2-page packet -> writes stub mastery result. KRAMOS owns the packet. Target week of June 15.
agreements_from_kramos.ownership_boundaries: Agreed as drafted (Viridian owns profiles + mastery; KRAMOS owns packets).

kramos_consumer_notes.kramos_ask: Post one fully-populated example profile payload (any one domain) to the location you want KRAMOS to read from. Unblocks consumer wiring and the June 15 dry-run at once.
kramer_owned_flags: lhum400 standards_source (Kramer's course objectives) is a Kramer input, not generable
June 23 LHUM400 start + KRAMOS next_action dates are targets; Kramer ratifies hard dates against a loaded Summer term
stress-test domain choice is Kyle + Kramer, untouched by KRAMOS

metadata.notes: This file is the single source of truth for Project X state. Both Viridian and KRAMOS read and write to it. Updates trigger the other system to query and adapt. KRAMOS added kramos_consumer_notes 2026-06-09 (additive; no Viridian fields altered except two KRAMOS-owned next_actions marked acknowledged).
